concepts.name: Imperative
concepts.description: A programming style where code specifies explicit steps that change program state and control execution flow.

subconcepts.name: Procedural
subconcepts.description: An imperative style where programs are organized as procedures or routines that operate on data and call each other.

subconcepts.name: Object-oriented
subconcepts.description: A programming style where programs are organized around objects that combine state, behavior, and interactions through methods.

subconcepts.name: Class-based
subconcepts.description: Object-oriented programming where objects are instances of classes, and classes define their structure, behavior, and inheritance relationships.

subconcepts.name: Object-based
subconcepts.description: Object-oriented programming that uses objects to encapsulate state and behavior, but does not rely on class inheritance or subtyping.

subconcepts.name: Prototype-based
subconcepts.description: Object-oriented programming that avoids classes and implements inheritance via cloning of instances

subconcepts.name: Data, context, and interaction
subconcepts.description: Paradigm that emphasizes mental models and run-time behavior of networks of objects, whose responsibilities are granted dynamically based on roles they play in interactions with other objects

concepts.name: Declarative
concepts.description: A programming style where code describes desired results, relationships, or constraints more than explicit step-by-step state changes.

subconcepts.name: Functional
subconcepts.description: A declarative style where computation is expressed through function application and composition, often avoiding mutable state and side effects.

subconcepts.name: Logic
subconcepts.description: A declarative style where programs are expressed as facts and rules, and computation is performed by querying or deriving logical consequences.

subconcepts.name: Reactive
subconcepts.description: A programming style where computations react to events, data streams, or changing values, propagating updates through dependent computations.

subconcepts.name: Constraint programming
subconcepts.description: A declarative style where relations between variables are expressed as constraints, and a solver searches for values that satisfy them.

subconcepts.name: Dataflow programming
subconcepts.description: A programming style where computation is modeled as data flowing through operations, and operations execute when their input data becomes available or changes.

subconcepts.name: Rule-based programming
subconcepts.description: A declarative style where behavior or inference is driven by a set of rules, often matching conditions against facts to derive conclusions or trigger actions.

concepts.name: Concurrent and Parallel programming
concepts.description: A programming style focused on structuring computations that overlap in time, run simultaneously, or coordinate multiple flows of execution.

subconcepts.name: Actor programming
subconcepts.description: A concurrent style where independent actors encapsulate state and communicate by sending messages rather than sharing mutable state directly.

subconcepts.name: Distributed programming
subconcepts.description: A programming style for systems whose components run on multiple networked machines and coordinate through communication protocols or message passing.

subconcepts.name: Parallel programming
subconcepts.description: A programming style where multiple computations are executed simultaneously to improve throughput, latency, or computational performance.

concepts.name: Generic programming
concepts.description: A programming style where algorithms and data structures are written in terms of type parameters, interfaces, traits, or concepts so they can be reused across many concrete types.

concepts.name: Metaprogramming
concepts.description: A programming style where programs generate, inspect, transform, or extend code or program structure, either at compile time, load time, or runtime.

concepts.name: Aspect-oriented programming
concepts.description: A programming style that separates cross-cutting concerns such as logging, authorization, tracing, or transactions into aspects that are applied across multiple program locations.

concepts.name: Event-driven programming
concepts.description: A programming style where control flow is driven by events such as user actions, messages, timers, I/O readiness, or signals, typically handled through callbacks, listeners, or event loops.

concepts.name: Data-oriented programming
concepts.description: A programming style that emphasizes data layout, data transformation, and cache-friendly processing, often separating data from behavior for performance or clarity.

concepts.name: Array programming
concepts.description: A programming style where operations are expressed over whole arrays, matrices, or tensors rather than explicit scalar loops, often used in numerical and data analysis code.
